explanation: General practice can evaluate common or non-specific symptoms and refer onward if needed.
guardrail: This is a general starting point; a doctor will confirm the actual issue during the visit.

keywords: wound;laceration;hernia;foreign object;swallowed object;bloated abdomen;torn nail;deep cut
explanation: These symptoms often require a surgical evaluation.
guardrail: Only a hands-on exam can confirm whether surgery is actually needed.

keywords: bad breath;drooling;wont chew;broken tooth;swollen gums;dental;tooth pain;mouth pain
explanation: Oral symptoms are best evaluated by dentistry.
guardrail: Dental pain can be more serious than it looks; do not delay if your pet stops eating.

keywords: itching;scratching;rash;hot spot;hair loss;skin;fur loss;flaky skin;red skin
explanation: Skin and coat symptoms are best evaluated by dermatology.
guardrail: Persistent scratching can also signal pain elsewhere and not just a skin issue.

keywords: seizure;seizures;collapse;collapsed;unconscious;unresponsive;difficulty breathing;cant breathe;cannot breathe;choking;heavy bleeding;uncontrolled bleeding;hit by car;poison;poisoned;toxic ingestion;straining to urinate
guardrail: Do not wait for a scheduled appointment; contact an emergency vet immediately.

keywords: coughing;fainting;exercise intolerance;rapid breathing;pale gums;heart murmur;short of breath
explanation: Coughing and fainting episodes can point to a heart-related issue.
guardrail: Breathing or fainting episodes can escalate quickly; seek care promptly.

keywords: limping;wont jump;stiff;joint pain;favoring a leg;cant put weight;lameness
explanation: Limping and joint stiffness are usually orthopedic in nature.
guardrail: Avoid strenuous activity until a vet has evaluated the leg or joint.

explanation: New lumps or growths should be evaluated to rule out something serious.
guardrail: Not every lump is cancer, but only testing can tell; do not wait too long to get it checked.
Sudden neurological changes should be seen quickly even if they seem to resolve.

keywords: eye discharge;red eye;squinting;cloudy eye;eye redness;pawing at eye
explanation: Eye symptoms are best handled by ophthalmology to protect vision.
guardrail: Eye injuries can worsen fast; do not wait if the eye looks painful or cloudy.

keywords: recovering from surgery;mobility issues;slow to heal;muscle weakness;post surgery;physical therapy
explanation: Rehabilitation supports recovery and mobility after injury or surgery.
guardrail: Rehab plans should be coordinated with whichever vet handled the original issue.

explanation: Sudden behavior changes are often evaluated by behavior specialists.
guardrail: Sudden behavior changes can also be a symptom of physical pain or illness and not just behavioral.

keywords: vomiting;diarrhea;weight loss;lethargy;not eating;loss of appetite;excessive thirst;excessive urination;tired
explanation: These general or systemic symptoms are usually evaluated by internal medicine first.
guardrail: This is a broad starting point; persistent symptoms need an in-person workup.

explanation: These symptoms typically require imaging to help diagnose.
guardrail: Imaging alone does not replace a full exam and follow-up care.
